scientific_integrity.detail: Scientific integrity held because the archived workflow stayed at the level of study planning, hypothesis framing, and experiment design rather than claiming completed results.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package remained on the planning side of the boundary and did not cross into clinical or diagnostic advice.

methodological_ground.detail: Methodological grounding was preserved through the documented inputs, transformations, and expected artifacts.

code_usability.detail: This package is packaging-first and output-first, not code-first, so code usability is treated as not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: The archived review left some room to tighten how Generates a structured prompt framework for clinical study protocols. Supports Diagnostic,... maps onto a finished protocol-style deliverable.

reliability.note: The package stayed structured, but the archived score suggests more consistency would help under sparse or stress-case inputs.

performance_context.note: No point loss was recorded for performance context in the legacy audit.

agent_usability.note: The planning path is understandable, but the archived score suggests a little more trigger clarity would help agents route into it faster.

human_usability.note: Human usability was softened by the legacy issue 'Minor polish before wide rollout'. No major defects found

security.note: Security scored well, though the archived review still left some room to make boundary language even more explicit.

maintainability.note: The package remains maintainable, though the archived review saw modest room to simplify or stabilize its planning logic.

agent_specific.note: Agent-specific quality remained high, with a small gap around determinism or edge-case prompting behavior.
